title: trifolium squarrosum subsp. squarrosum
intro: Trifolium squarrosum subsp. squarrosum, also known as the broad-leaved red clover, is a subspecies of the red clover plant, characterized by its broad and hairy leaves. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Trifolium squarrosum subsp. squarrosum include reducing symptoms of menopause, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Trifolium squarrosum subsp. squarrosum is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to promote overall health and well-being.
